message.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze the input. This is a single round/part called "Flap" with title "Button hole" and raw instruction: "Now we will work our button hole."

This is a non-stitch instruction - it's essentially a note or bookkeeping instruction that tells the user what's happening next. It doesn't produce any stitches.

Let me think about how to encode this. The instruction "Now we will work our button hole." is purely informational - it's telling the user what's about to happen. It doesn't contain any crochet action.

I think this should be a `note` statement since it's purely informational. Alternatively, it could be a `bookkeeping` operation with some action tag. But since there's no actual action happening, a note seems more appropriate.

Actually, looking at the IR spec more carefully: "If the input round contains only a non-stitch instruction, emit a single `operation` with `semantics = bookkeeping` and an appropriate `actionTag`."

But this really is just a note - "Now we will work our button hole." There's no action to perform. Let me use a note statement since it's purely informational commentary.

Wait, let me reconsider. The spec says for non-stitch instructions, emit an operation with bookkeeping. But this is really just a note/comment. Let me go with `note` kind since it's purely informational text with no action.

Let me construct the IR:

- `kind`: "note"
- `sourceText`: "Now we will work our button hole."
- `note`: { `message`: "Now we will work our button hole.", `sourceText`: "Now we will work our button hole.", `emitAsAction`: false }
- `operation`: null
- `repeat`: null
- `conditional`: null

`expectedProducedStitches`: null (no stitches are produced)
`title`: "Button hole"

Let me finalize this.

function.arguments: {"rounds": [{"title": "Button hole", "sourceText": "Now we will work our button hole.", "expectedProducedStitches": null, "body": {"statements": [{"kind": "note", "sourceText": "Now we will work our button hole.", "operation": null, "repeat": null, "conditional": null, "note": {"message": "Now we will work our button hole.", "sourceText": "Now we will work our button hole.", "emitAsAction": false}}], "sourceText": null, "normalizationNote": null}}]}
function.name: return_crochet_ir_atomization
